serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Arabikki pork sausage is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one full sausage link as typically served as a side dish or snack.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork sausage, fresh, cooked (SR Legacy ID 10948)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No approved-source entry exists for 'Arabikki' branded Korean pork sausage. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork sausage (cooked) as the closest approved-source equivalent. Sodium is retained from the input record (650 mg/100g) as it reflects a Korean-seasoned processed sausage with higher sodium than the USDA baseline; this is documented in notes. All other macro values are cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy pork sausage cooked data and are consistent with the input record values.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Arabikki Korean pork sausage. GI estimated at 28 based on the analogous food 'sausage' (GI ~28) reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables, University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (28 × 4.0) / 100 = 1.12, rounded to 1.1. Low GI is consistent with the very low carbohydrate content (4 g/100g) of this processed pork sausage.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 650 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g high-concern threshold for processed meats
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Arabikki pork sausage due to its sodium content of 650 mg/100 g; even a single 100 g serving delivers 32.5% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and the additional saturated fat (8 g/100 g) further contributes to arterial stiffness and elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (4 g/100 g) minimises glycaemic impact, but high saturated fat (8 g/100 g) and processed-meat classification raise insulin resistance risk with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Arabikki pork sausage has a low glycaemic index (~28) and minimal carbohydrate load (GL ~1.1 per 100 g serving), making it unlikely to spike blood glucose acutely; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit frequency of consumption because the high saturated fat content (8 g/100 g) is associated with worsening insulin resistance, and the high sodium (650 mg/100 g) increases cardiovascular risk, which is already elevated in this population.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), sodium (650 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (50 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Arabikki pork sausage is a high-concern food for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its combination of saturated fat at 8 g/100 g (40% of the 20 g daily limit), trans fat at 0.2 g/100 g, sodium at 650 mg/100 g, and cholesterol at 50 mg/100 g; regular consumption of processed red meat is independently associated with increased risk of coronary heart disease and should be avoided or strictly limited in this population.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Processed red meat with saturated fat at 8 g/100 g exceeding the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; arachidonic acid from pork amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokines
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Arabikki pork sausage as processed pork is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways, and the saturated fat content of 8 g/100 g directly exceeds the 5 g/100 g threshold associated with increased inflammatory cytokine production in RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Arabikki Pork Sausage (아라비끼 돼지고기 소시지) is a Korean-style processed pork sausage sold and served in Singapore, typically grilled or pan-fried until the casing is lightly charred and the interior is fully cooked. The name 'Arabikki' is a Korean-market brand or style designation for a seasoned pork sausage with a savoury, mildly spiced flavour profile characteristic of Korean processed meat products. The texture is firm and juicy with a snappy casing, and the flavour is rich, salty, and umami-forward from the pork fat and seasoning blend. It is commonly served as a side dish (banchan-style) or as a standalone snack, and may be accompanied by rice, kimchi, or dipping sauces. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 280 kcal, 12 g protein, 24 g fat (of which 8 g is saturated fat and 0.2 g is trans fat), 4 g carbohydrate, 1 g sugar, and 650 mg sodium. The very low carbohydrate content results in a low glycaemic index (~28) and glycaemic load (~1.1 per serving), making it unlikely to cause acute blood glucose spikes. However, the high saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and processed-meat classification make it a high-concern food for hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis, and a moderate-concern food for Type 2 Diabetes due to insulin resistance risk from chronic saturated fat intake. It is not suitable for halal or kosher diets and is not vegetarian or vegan.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Korean pork sausage?
Is Korean sausage high in sodium?
Is Arabikki sausage suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of pork sausage?

confidence_reason: No approved primary source (HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central) contains an exact entry for 'Arabikki' branded Korean pork sausage. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked pork sausage as the closest approved-source equivalent, with sodium retained from the input record at 650 mg/100 g reflecting the Korean-seasoned formulation. GI is estimated from the analogous sausage entry in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork sausage (cooked) as no exact approved-source entry exists for Arabikki branded Korean pork sausage. Sodium value of 650 mg/100 g is retained from the input record as it is consistent with Korean-seasoned processed sausage formulations and exceeds the USDA baseline, reflecting additional seasoning. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Korean-branded processed pork sausage are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): processed pork sausage contains no plant-derived fibre; this is consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for cooked pork sausage. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): pork sausage contains no vitamin C per USDA SR Legacy data. 4. GI estimated at 28 from analogous sausage entry in Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for Arabikki pork sausage exists in approved sources. 5. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) is a forbidden delivery/app platform and has been replaced with the approved USDA FoodData Central source. 6. Ethnic tags 'Bibimbap' and 'Korean Fried Chicken' from the input record have been removed as they are unrelated to this product.
